task_completion.description: The CT Cranium DICOM dataset is loaded in InVesalius 3.
task_completion.visual_evidence: InVesalius 3 interface is visible with axial, coronal, and sagittal views showing the CT Cranium scans.

task_completion.description: A bone segmentation mask is created with threshold 226-3071 HU.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The threshold tool in InVesalius shows values around 226 to 3071 HU (or the 'Bone' preset is selected), and a colored mask overlays the bone structures in the 2D slices.

task_completion.description: A 3D surface of the skull is generated.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 3D viewer pane in InVesalius displays a 3D rendered model of the segmented skull.

task_completion.description: The high-quality 3D surface is exported as skull_best.stl.
task_completion.visual_evidence: An 'Export 3D surface' save dialog or a file manager window shows the file 'skull_best.stl' being saved to or existing in the /home/ga/Documents/ directory.

task_completion.description: The lower-resolution 3D surface is exported as skull_lowres.stl.
task_completion.visual_evidence: An 'Export 3D surface' save dialog or a file manager window shows the file 'skull_lowres.stl' being saved to or existing in the /home/ga/Documents/ directory. There may also be evidence of the agent selecting a lower surface quality preset before this export.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the InVesalius 3 GUI to perform the tasks.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent clicking through InVesalius menus, threshold panels, and 3D surface generation options, rather than just running terminal commands to bypass the UI.

integrity.description: The STL files were generated by InVesalius, not created as dummy files.
integrity.visual_evidence: The export process is visible via InVesalius's own 'Export 3D surface' dialog, rather than using terminal commands like `touch` or a text editor.

integrity.description: Both surfaces were created and exported in the same InVesalius session.
integrity.visual_evidence: The InVesalius application remains open with the same dataset loaded throughout the process of generating and exporting both the high and low-resolution surfaces.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Target files: /home/ga/Documents/skull_best.stl and /home/ga/Documents/skull_lowres.stl. The high-quality file must have at least 1.2x more triangles than the low-resolution file, and both must have >5000 triangles. While the VLM cannot count triangles, it should look for evidence that the agent intentionally created two surfaces of different qualities (e.g., selecting different quality presets in the 'Create 3D surface' dialog) and exported them to the correct paths.
